status: CLINICIAN-ATTESTED clinical sign-off (reviewer KL, 2026-07-13): the 8 adult, sex-agnostic reference ranges present (incl. critical thresholds) are clinically valid for adult sex-agnostic use by the investigation parser. NOT YET patient-facing — regulatory (TGA) sign-off (FL-50) + sex/age-specific + broader analyte coverage + a live FHIR lab source (FL-32) still required; version stays dev-tagged until the full sign-off clears. (Was: DEV/SYNTHETIC-ONLY.)

attestation.recorded_by: claude-fable-5 (agent, on the clinician's explicit written attestation in-session)
attestation.statement: Clinician reviewed and attests the 8 adult sex-agnostic reference ranges present (troponin I, creatinine, potassium, sodium, haemoglobin, WCC, CRP, glucose), including the low/high and critical_low/critical_high thresholds, as clinically valid for adult sex-agnostic use by the deterministic investigation parser. There is no separate 'authoritative set' to swap in — this attestation makes the current set the clinically-signed-off set for that scope. Clinical sign-off only; sex/age-specific ranges and broader analyte coverage remain a separate expansion; regulatory sign-off not provided here; no live lab source until fhir-broker goes live (FL-32).
attestation.scope: the 8 analytes in this file (lab-reference-ranges, dev v0.1.0), adult sex-agnostic

units_note: Each value must be supplied in the analyte's listed unit. A null threshold means that band does not apply to the analyte (e.g. troponin and CRP have no clinically meaningful low band).
